intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The registry indicates P1-P4, but the visual evidence shows only two visible containers: one yellow pot (P1) and one black pot (P3/P4). The white rabbit anchor is present in the black pot, but the plant specimens themselves are absent or completely desiccated.

inventory_reconciliation.New_Introductions: None observed; only debris and soil remain.

plant_audit.P1_String_of_Nickels: No visible foliage. Soil appears dark and potentially waterlogged or devoid of life.
plant_audit.P3_Pothos: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos specimen is missing or entirely consumed/withered. Only soil and white debris (likely perlite or mineral deposits) remain.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Mexican_Mint_Silver_Guest: These specimens are not present in the frame. Declared Systemic Loss.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dark, appears saturated. No signs of active growth or moss.
biome_observations.debris: White particulate matter (perlite) scattered on the surface of the black pot. No fungal blooms detected.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no organic matter outside of the pots.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, there is zero change in the biological status of the pots. The environment is static and devoid of living plant matter. The 'Rested State' and 'Current' images confirm the absence of any recovery or new growth.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. The absence of foliage in all observed pots suggests a total failure of the indoor botanical system. The plants are either dead or have been removed.
vision_report.anomalies: The primary anomaly is the complete absence of the registered plants (Pothos, String of Nickels, Mexican Mint) despite the presence of the pots and the scale anchor.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to map the current visual data against the provided registry. Upon validation, I confirmed that the biome is in a state of total systemic loss. The pots are present, but the biological occupants are missing. The soil in the black pot (P3) remains static with no signs of life, and the yellow pot (P1) shows no evidence of the String of Nickels. The environment is currently a sterile, non-productive setup.
